title: Building Better Study Habits
instructions: Fill in the missing letters in the paragraph.
passageTemplate: Good study habits help students stay calm and make steady progress. Before starting, they {{1}} a simple plan and {{2}} one realistic goal. During study time, they {{3}} phones so attention stays on the task. They also {{4}} the work into short parts and {{5}} breaks at regular times. When a topic feels hard, they {{6}} notes in their own words and ask a teacher for {{7}} examples. At the end, they {{8}} on what they learned, {{9}} questions they still have, and save useful ideas in one organized {{10}} for future review and steady improvement.

gaps.explanation: Create means to make a simple plan before studying.
Choose means to select one realistic goal.
Eliminate means to remove distractions such as phones.
Break means to divide the work into shorter parts.
Take breaks at regular times helps students stay focused.
Rewrite means to put notes into your own words.
Clear examples make a hard topic easier to understand.
Reflect means to think carefully about what you learned.
Note means to write down the questions you still have.
A folder helps students store useful ideas in one place.

title: Planning a Community Recycling Project
instructions: Fill in the missing letters in the paragraph.
passageTemplate: A community recycling project can reduce waste and build shared responsibility. Volunteers {{1}} clean plastic, {{2}} cardboard, and {{3}} meetings with local shops to explain the goals. The team has to {{4}} materials by type, {{5}} residents with posters, and {{6}} useful items whenever possible. They also {{7}} schools so children learn early habits. Careful planning helps the group {{8}} trust, {{9}} the process, and {{10}} the program every month. Students also record how much material they collect, because the numbers show whether the project is growing or needs a new plan. When neighbours see cleaner streets and fuller bins, they often join the effort and keep the habit going.

gaps.explanation: Collect means to gather clean plastic for recycling.
Pack means to place cardboard together for sorting.
Arrange means to plan meetings with local shops.
Sort means to separate materials by type.
Motivate means to encourage residents to join in.
Reuse means to use useful items again.
Involve means to include schools in the project.
Build trust is important when a project depends on local support.
Track means to follow the process month by month.
Continue means to keep the program going every month.

title: How Birds Share Information
instructions: Fill in the missing letters in the paragraph.
passageTemplate: Birds communicate in more ways than people first notice. Some species {{1}} quietly when they are alone, while others {{2}} out to warn a group about danger. A bird may {{3}} its call when the weather changes or when a rival enters the area. Parents also {{4}} chicks with repeated sounds, and young birds {{5}} familiar patterns before using them on their own. Even movement can {{6}} a message, because a raised feather or a steady dance may signal attention. By {{7}} closely, researchers can {{8}} how birds share space, find mates, and protect {{9}}. Their sounds are not random; they {{10}} the life of the flock.

gaps.explanation: Sing fits the idea of birds making quiet sounds.
Call means to make a warning sound.
Alter means to change the call in different situations.
Respond means to answer or react to chicks.
Learn means to pick up familiar patterns before using them.
Convey means to send a message without words.
Watching closely helps researchers notice the signals.
Explain means to show how the birds share information.
Nests are the places birds protect.
Support means to help sustain the life of the flock.
